saints.en: St. Great Martyr James the Persian. Ven. Palladius

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 мч., 3 прп., Сл., І нині: богород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл.: прп., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прп. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мч. і прп.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар мч. (2), Сл.: прп., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прп. Катизми чергові, сідальні Октоїха. Канон Октоїха, мч. і прп. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, мч., Сл.: прп., І нині: богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл.: прп., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря прп. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мч. і прп.
Часи: на 1-му – тропар дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му – мч. та 9-му – прп.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – 2 Тим. 290 зач.; 1, 1-2. 8-18. Єв. – Лк. 95 зач.; 19, 12-28.

troparion_en.text: You astounded all, long-suffering James, by enduring horrible tortures with great patience. / As the evil assembly performed the slaughter, / you uttered prayers of thanksgiving to the Lord. / Through your sufferings you received your crown, / and came to the throne of the heavenly King, Christ God. / Entreat Him to save our souls!

kontakion_en.text: You listened to your faithful wife / and contemplated the judgment of God, holy James; / you despised the threats and commands of the Persians, / accepting the cutting of your body as though you were a vine. / Therefore you were revealed as a martyr worthy of honor.

troparion.text: You astounded all, long-suffering James, by enduring horrible tortures with great patience. / As the evil assembly performed the slaughter, / you uttered prayers of thanksgiving to the Lord. / Through your sufferings you received your crown, / and came to the throne of the heavenly King, Christ God. / Entreat Him to save our souls!

kontakion.text: You listened to your faithful wife / and contemplated the judgment of God, holy James; / you despised the threats and commands of the Persians, / accepting the cutting of your body as though you were a vine. / Therefore you were revealed as a martyr worthy of honor.

troparion.text: Pleasing God in monasticism, O God-bearing Father Palladius,  / you sought Him through prayer and fasting, /  and were the pure abode of the Spirit, shining upon the faithful with the rays of virtues,  / which enlighten all who revere you.  / Glory to Christ Who glorified you.  / Glory to Him Who strengthened you.  / Glory to Him Who, through you, grants healing to all.

oca_saints.name: Saint James the Wonderworker, Bishop of Rostov

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father James, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: As venerable and proven ascetics, as honorable and renowned martyrs,  / and as models of the desert life, O most exalted Fathers, /  we sing hymns of praise to you,  / for you have trampled upon serpents.

oca_saints.name: Repose of Venerable Diodorus, Igoumen of George Hill

troparion.text: Aflame with spiritual desire, and rejecting worldly vanity,  / you became attached to the One God by love and you sought Him with all your soul.  / You went into the inner wilderness, and settled by the water,  / where you remained in tears and labors for many years with great patience.  / You led an angelic life, and guided by the Divine mind,  / you gathered a flock of monks, O wise one.  /  Do not cease to visit them, Venerable Diódoros our Father,  / but entreat the All-Holy Trinity to deliver us from all evils, and to save our souls.

kontakion.text: From your youth, O Venerable one,  / you exhausted your carnal desires by fasting and prayers.  / Taking up your cross, you followed Christ.  / Therefore, you hastened joyously on the path to Heaven.  / Now as you stand before the Holy Trinity with all the Saints,  / visit your flock and remember those who revere your holy memory, that we may cry out thankfully to you:  /  “Rejoice, O godly-wise Diódoros, our instructor in the desert.”

oca_saints.name: Commemoration of the Weeping Icon of the Mother of God “of the Sign” at Novgorod

troparion.text: Having obtained you as an unassailable rampart and a source of miracles, O Most Pure Theotokos,   / we your servants vanquish the assaults of enemies.   / Therefore we entreat you: “Grant peace to your city, and great mercy to our souls.”

kontakion.text: We celebrate the appearance of your revered Icon of the Sign, O Theotokos, whereby you have granted your people a wondrous victory over the enemies of your city. Therefore, we cry to you with faith: “Rejoice, O Virgin, the boast of Christians.”

oca_saints.name: “Kursk-Root” Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Having obtained you as an unassailable rampart and a source of miracles, O Most Pure Theotokos,   / we your servants vanquish the assaults of enemies.   / Therefore we entreat you: “Grant peace to your city, and great mercy to our souls.”

kontakion.text: We celebrate the appearance of your revered Icon of the Sign, O Theotokos, whereby you have granted your people a wondrous victory over the enemies of your city. Therefore, we cry to you with faith: “Rejoice, O Virgin, the boast of Christians.”

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God of Abalaka

troparion.text: Today your Icon shines brightly like an unsetting sun, O Most Pure One,  /  appearing miraculously in the heavens to a widow in the village of Abalak.   / And bowing before it, we humbly cry out to the Theotokos:   / “O Sovereign Lady hymned by all, grant peace to your abode,   / and to our souls great mercy.”

kontakion.text: Reverently bowing down before the Abalak Icon of our Sovereign Lady,  / with humility we cry out:  / “Save us by your prayers, O Most Pure Virgin.”

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God of Tsarskoe Selo

troparion.text: Having obtained you as an unassailable rampart and a source of miracles, O Most Pure Theotokos,   / we your servants vanquish the assaults of enemies.   / Therefore we entreat you: “Grant peace to your city, and great mercy to our souls.”

oca_saints.name: “Seraphim-Ponetaevka” Icon of the Mother of God
